body.title: CLOSED: narrative turn log
body.result: narrative_turn.log + log_cascade_event/log_memory_retrieval in forensic_logger, wired into cascade/cortex/pipeline
body.description:
Add a per-turn narrative log that surfaces whether Igor's cognition is working or full of noise. Without this, cognition quality is only intuitable from the console.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/turn_pipeline.py, new wild_igor/igor/cognition/narrative_logger.py
**Design rules:** docs-live-in-code; no-sqlite (Postgres or flat-file only)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: one log entry per TurnPipeline.run_turn call, written to datacenter_logs/igor/cognition/narrative_turn.log. Each entry: turn_id, timestamp, channel, raw user_input, top retrieved memories (id + narrative[:150]), cascade path taken (level names + status), voice context summary (first 300 chars), final response text. Out of scope: log rotation, retention policy, UI viewer.
**Test plan:** Invoke TurnPipeline with a StubCortex; assert log file contains an entry with correct fields (turn_id, user_input, cascade path, response).
**Pass condition:** After a real Igor turn, narrative_turn.log has a readable entry showing what was retrieved and what was assembled — visible in 'tail -1 narrative_turn.log' without needing to decode any binary or query Postgres.
